model_summary: REPAIRED gVAR-compatible observation model with 6 criterion variables representing nightmare-related sleep disruption and 6 predictor variables spanning cognitive-behavioral sleep strategies and physiological/environmental supports. Adjusted for momentary sampling consistency, top-edge sparsity alignment with dense grids, and updated edge-optimization to reflect higher-valued incoming connections where appropriate.
variable_selection_notes: Case focuses on nightmare frequency/intensity, anticipatory fear of sleep, autonomic arousal on awakening, post-awakening activation, and difficulty resuming sleep. Predictor set covers sleep routines, sleep environment, presleep downshift, breathing/relaxation, CBT sleep skills, and imagery rescripting. All variables are non-pharmacological and suitable for digital data capture.

momentary_ema.notes: Momentary assessments for all six criteria during daytime and evening prompts; align with sleep-wake cycle.

daily_diary.notes: Evening diary capturing perceived sleep quality and nightmare-related stress.

passive_streams.notes: No passive streams enabled in initial iteration.

design_recommendations.gvar_readiness_notes: Initial readiness moderate; ensure high compliance with EMA prompts.
design_recommendations.variance_support_notes: Expect high within-person variability in nightmare frequency and arousal; plan for detrending if needed.
design_recommendations.missingness_risk_notes: Implement reminder prompts and fallback diary entries to mitigate missing data.

measurement.notes: Prompt user to indicate number of nightmare-triggered awakenings since last prompt; scale reflects frequency.
Capture subjective vividness of dream content per awakening.

criteria_variables.label: Anticipatory fear of sleep / reluctance to go to bed

measurement.notes: Subjective arousal indicators (sweating, heart rate) at awakening.

criteria_variables.label: Difficulty resuming sleep after nightmare awakenings

measurement.notes: Self-reported difficulty returning to sleep after awakenings.

predictor_variables.label: Sleep Routine/CBT-like bedtime structuring
predictor_variables.mechanism_rationale: Structured routines reduce sleep-associated conditioned arousal and improve sleep onset.
predictor_variables.intervention_examples_safe: Imagery rehearsal, fixed wind-down, consistent bed time.

measurement.notes: assesses adherence to bedtime routine and perceived effectiveness.

predictor_variables.label: Sleep Environment Optimization (sleep-conducive room factors)
predictor_variables.mechanism_rationale: Air quality, temperature, light, and noise influence sleep onset and nocturnal arousal.
predictor_variables.intervention_examples_safe: Optimize lighting, reduce noise, cool dark room.

predictor_variables.label: Presleep Downshift/Rituals (downshift from wakefulness to sleep)
predictor_variables.mechanism_rationale: Relaxation and mental unloading reduce pre-sleep arousal and nightmare anticipation.
predictor_variables.intervention_examples_safe: Breathing, progressive muscle relaxation, sensory downshift.

measurement.notes: Assess perceived effectiveness of presleep downshift.

expected_effects.rationale: Better presleep routine reduces anticipatory fear.

predictor_variables.label: Breathing/Detachment during pre-sleep for arousal downshift
predictor_variables.mechanism_rationale: Slow breathing and detachment reduce cognitive arousal before sleep.
predictor_variables.intervention_examples_safe: paced breathing, body scan, cognitive detachment.

measurement.notes: Capture perceived effectiveness of calming breathing sequences.

predictor_variables.label: Sleep-related CBT skills training / sleep appraisals
predictor_variables.mechanism_rationale: Cognitive restructuring and behavioral experiments reduce sleep-related worry and nightmare threat beliefs.
predictor_variables.intervention_examples_safe: CBT-I components: stimulus control, cognitive restructuring, sleep restriction.

measurement.notes: Assess engagement and perceived efficacy of CBT sleep strategies.

expected_effects.rationale: Enhanced sleep preparation and reduced sleep anxiety.

predictor_variables.label: Nightmare imagery rehearsal / rescripting techniques
predictor_variables.mechanism_rationale: Imagery rehearsal reduces nightmare frequency/intensity through cognitive rehearsal and mastery.

measurement.notes: Assess frequency and perceived efficacy of imagery rehearsal.

expected_effects.rationale: Aids in reducing nightmare vividness via rehearsal exposure.

expected_collinearity_risks: Possible collinearity between C01 and C02 when reporting frequent awakenings and high intensity
Overlap between C04 and C05 arousal items if not time-anchored
expected_floor_ceiling_risks: C03/C05 may saturate at high values if anticipatory fear dominates
variance_enhancement_suggestions: Include alternating day-of-week prompts to capture weekly variation
excluded_or_deprioritized_candidates: No pharmacological predictors included in initial model
diagnostics.gvar_feasibility_summary: Feasible with 12 nodes; EMA-based criterion measures and a mix of predictor modalities supported.

safety_notes: This is not a diagnosis or treatment recommendation. If distress worsens or there are safety concerns (e.g., suicidality, intensified nightmares with self-harm risk), seek immediate professional help. Provide crisis resources and escalation pathways in the app.
